teachingPoint: IR-T613-1 — T613 GigAcceptanceLockGateway MUST enforce three sequential guards: BOLA at ORDER 1 (client owns gig), SETNX at ORDER 2 (exclusive acceptance lock), OCC at ORDER 3 (bid still OPEN). Missing BOLA enables cross-tenant gig hijacking. Missing SETNX enables double-acceptance race. Missing OCC enables stale-bid acceptance after withdrawal. All three guards are MACHINE-FIXED — order cannot be configured.
positiveExample: T613: validateBOLA(gigPosting.clientTenantId === ALS.tenantId) at ORDER 1 → SETNX('gig-accept-lock:{gigId}') at ORDER 2 → OCC bid status check at ORDER 3 → storeDocument(audit) at ORDER 4 → enqueue(GigAccepted) at ORDER 5.
negativeExample: T613 skips BOLA — any tenant can accept gigs belonging to another tenant. Or T613 skips SETNX — two concurrent accept requests both pass and double-accept. Or T613 accepts after bid is WITHDRAWN — stale acceptance of an invalid bid.
discriminatingConstraint: BOLA ORDER 1 before SETNX, SETNX before OCC, OCC before storeDocument. Any reorder = BUILD_FAILURE. Extends CART-LOCK-SETNX-001 from FLOW-16.
keywords: BOLA ORDER 1 SETNX gig-accept-lock OCC bid status GigAccepted GigAcceptanceFailed T613 GigAcceptanceLockGateway three-guard

teachingPoint: IR-T614-1 — T614 MilestoneContractManager MUST define CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS as a compile-time constant. Immutable fields (clientId, contractTotal, gigId, freelancerId) must never be updatable after contract activation. Any write attempt to an immutable field MUST be rejected at ORDER 1 with no storage attempt. Sum validation at ORDER 2 ensures milestones always equal contractTotal — preventing escrow funding gaps.
positiveExample: T614: const CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS = ['clientId','contractTotal','gigId','freelancerId'] at module level → ORDER 1 immutable field check → if immutable: emit ContractFieldImmutable; return → ORDER 2 sum validation milestones.reduce(sum) === contractTotal → ORDER 3 storeDocumentWithOCC → ORDER 4 audit → ORDER 5 emit FreelancerContractActivated.
negativeExample: T614 allows contractTotal to be updated — attacker changes total mid-engagement to pocket escrow funds. Or T614 skips sum validation — milestones sum to 80% of total, 20% escrow gap is never funded. Or T614 uses runtime lookup for immutable fields — attacker removes contractTotal from the list.
discriminatingConstraint: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS compile-time constant at ORDER 1, sum validation at ORDER 2, OCC write at ORDER 3. Runtime immutable-field list or skipped sum validation = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: CONTRACT_IMMUTABLE_FIELDS compile-time sum validation OCC milestones FreelancerContractActivated ContractFieldImmutable MilestoneSumMismatch T614 MilestoneContractManager

teachingPoint: IR-T615-1 — T615 DeliveryGateEscrowController MUST validate delivery.status === SUBMITTED before any funds movement. A client cannot release escrow without an accepted deliverable — this is the primary protection against fraudulent release. Dispute path uses updateDocument(status:DISPUTED) ONLY — no funds movement. LIFO compensation ensures the most recent operation (escrow release) is reversed before restoring gig status.
positiveExample: T615 release: validate delivery at ORDER 1 → storeDocument(milestone:RELEASED) at ORDER 2 → audit at ORDER 3 → emit MilestoneReleased at ORDER 4. Dispute: updateDocument(DISPUTED) only. LIFO: [REFUND_MILESTONE, RESTORE_GIG_STATUS] registered forward, executed reversed.
negativeExample: T615 releases funds without checking delivery — client clicks release on funded milestone without submitting work. Or T615 uses FIFO compensation — REFUND_MILESTONE runs first but RESTORE_GIG_STATUS fails, leaving gig status unreleased with refunded funds. Or T615 deletes milestone on dispute — audit trail destroyed.
discriminatingConstraint: Delivery gate at ORDER 1 before funds movement. LIFO [REFUND_MILESTONE, RESTORE_GIG_STATUS]. No deleteDocument. Release before delivery or FIFO = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: delivery gate before release LIFO REFUND_MILESTONE RESTORE_GIG_STATUS MilestoneReleased MilestoneDisputed T615 DeliveryGateEscrowController updateDocument DISPUTED

teachingPoint: IR-T616-1 — T616 FreelancerReviewWriter MUST enforce single-direction reviews. Direction check at ORDER 1 (CLIENT_TO_FREELANCER or FREELANCER_TO_CLIENT). Duplicate direction check at ORDER 2 prevents reputation inflation. Reviews are append-only — no updateDocument after submission. Comment text MUST NOT appear in the audit record because it may contain PII that cannot be in PLATFORM_ONLY audit exports.
positiveExample: T616: direction check at ORDER 1 → duplicate searchDocuments at ORDER 2 → storeDocument(review, PRIVATE) at ORDER 3 → storeDocument(audit, NO comment) at ORDER 4 → emit FreelancerReviewSubmitted at ORDER 5. Audit record contains reviewId, direction, rating — comment excluded.
negativeExample: T616 allows two reviews in CLIENT_TO_FREELANCER direction — reputation inflation attack. Or T616 includes comment in PLATFORM_ONLY audit record — operator export leaks user PII. Or T616 uses updateDocument on reviews — reviewers retract negative feedback after payment.
discriminatingConstraint: Direction at ORDER 1, duplicate check at ORDER 2, append-only, comment excluded from audit. Duplicate direction allowed or comment in audit = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: CLIENT_TO_FREELANCER FREELANCER_TO_CLIENT direction check duplicate append-only comment excluded PLATFORM_ONLY FreelancerReviewSubmitted DuplicateReviewRejected T616 FreelancerReviewWriter

teachingPoint: IR-T615-2 — LIFO compensation at milestone granularity is the only safe compensation strategy for freelancer escrow. Each milestone is an independent escrow unit. LIFO ensures the most recently started operation (escrow release) is reversed before restoring the gig status — preventing a race window where the gig is available for re-acceptance but funds are still in transit. Compensating all milestones when one fails is incorrect — it reverses successful prior milestones.
positiveExample: T615 LIFO chain: const ESCROW_COMPENSATION_CHAIN = ['REFUND_MILESTONE', 'RESTORE_GIG_STATUS'] — registered forward. Executor: [...ESCROW_COMPENSATION_CHAIN].reverse() → [RESTORE_GIG_STATUS, REFUND_MILESTONE]. RESTORE_GIG_STATUS runs first (gig not available during refund), then REFUND_MILESTONE.
negativeExample: T615 FIFO compensation — REFUND_MILESTONE runs first, then RESTORE_GIG_STATUS fails. Gig has released funds but is stuck in RELEASED state, blocking future acceptance. Or T615 compensates all milestones — reverses completed milestones that had no failure.
discriminatingConstraint: LIFO at milestone granularity — registered forward [REFUND, RESTORE], executed reversed [RESTORE, REFUND]. FIFO or all-milestone = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: LIFO REFUND_MILESTONE RESTORE_GIG_STATUS milestone granularity compensation chain EscrowCompensationStarted T615 DeliveryGateEscrowController reverse FIFO incorrect

teachingPoint: IR-T616-2 — Review audit in T616 MUST be append-only AND comment-excluded. Append-only ensures reputation signal provenance is tamper-evident — no review can be retracted or modified after submission. Comment exclusion from audit ensures platform operators cannot access user-written free text through audit exports — preventing PII leakage. The comment is stored in the review record with PRIVATE scope but excluded from the audit record.
positiveExample: T616 audit: storeDocument(REVIEW_AUDIT_INDEX, { reviewId, direction, rating, reviewerId, targetId, submittedAt } — no comment field) → no updateDocument on review records ever. Review stored separately with PRIVATE scope including comment.
negativeExample: T616 audit includes comment: { reviewId, direction, rating, comment: 'Payment was slow' } — comment appears in platform audit exports with PLATFORM_ONLY scope, leaking PII. Or T616 calls updateDocument on review record — reviewer changes 1-star to 5-star after being paid for a refund.
discriminatingConstraint: Append-only (storeDocument only, no updateDocument). Comment excluded from audit record. Any comment in platform audit or updateDocument on review = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: append-only review audit comment excluded PLATFORM_ONLY PII FreelancerReviewSubmitted T616 FreelancerReviewWriter no updateDocument PRIVATE scope
